headline: Attackers tore through enterprise software—an Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day, a critical Splunk flaw, and a decade-long China-linked login backdoor—while Washington forced Anthropic's top AI models offline.

emerging_trends.subject: Enterprise Targeting
emerging_trends.text: Attackers concentrated on enterprise backbone software, with Oracle PeopleSoft and Splunk Enterprise flaws each exposing hundreds of organizations through a single unauthenticated weakness.

emerging_trends.subject: AI Crossfire
emerging_trends.text: Generative AI sat on both sides of the day's news as Google sued a network for weaponizing Gemini in phishing and the U.S. forced Anthropic's frontier models offline over national-security fears.

emerging_trends.subject: Supply Chain
emerging_trends.text: Attackers hijacked more than 400 Arch Linux AUR packages the same week npm moved to disable dependency scripts by default, putting the open-source build pipeline at the center of both attack and defense.

emerging_trends.subject: Decade-Deep Access
emerging_trends.text: Velvet Ant's ten-year login backdoor and a freshly patched decade-old phpBB authentication bypass both show how long undetected access can hide inside trusted code.

topics.title: Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day
topics.last_24h: The ShinyHunters crew exploited an Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day across hundreds of organizations, stealing gigabytes of data and hitting American universities hardest.
topics.summary: A critical flaw in Oracle's PeopleSoft ERP software lets attackers reach records without authentication. ShinyHunters capitalized fast, draining data from hundreds of victims, with higher-education institutions absorbing the worst of it. Organizations running PeopleSoft should apply Oracle's emergency fix and hunt for signs of bulk data export.

sources.title: ShinyHunters Uses Oracle Zero-Day to Rampage Higher Ed
PeopleSoft 0-day affecting hundreds of organizations steals gigabytes of data

topics.title: Arch Linux AUR Package Hijack
topics.last_24h: Attackers seized more than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository and rewrote their build scripts to drop a credential stealer and an eBPF rootkit.
topics.summary: The malware is a Rust binary that harvests developer secrets and access tokens from any machine that builds a poisoned package, and with root it loads an eBPF rootkit to hide itself. The AUR is Arch Linux's community-maintained collection, separate from the official repositories and unvetted by the project. The episode landed the same week npm announced that version 12 will stop running dependency scripts by default, a direct counter to this style of attack. Developers should rebuild affected machines and rotate every exposed credential.

sources.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
Over 400 Arch Linux packages compromised to push rootkit, infostealer
NPM 12 Will Change Script Execution Behavior to Prevent Supply Chain Attacks

topics.title: Critical Splunk Enterprise RCE
topics.last_24h: Splunk patched CVE-2026-20253, a 9.8-severity flaw that lets an unauthenticated attacker create or truncate files and run code remotely.
topics.summary: The bug affects Splunk Enterprise versions below 10.2.4 and 10.0.7, where an unauthenticated user can perform arbitrary file operations that escalate to remote code execution. Splunk's deep footprint inside security operations centers makes the platform a high-value target. Administrators should upgrade immediately.

sources.title: Critical Splunk Enterprise Flaw Lets Attackers Run Code Without Authentication

topics.title: Velvet Ant Decade-Long Login Backdoor
topics.last_24h: Sygnia revealed that the China-linked Velvet Ant group hid inside a target's Linux login software for nearly ten years, controlling the entire authentication stack.
topics.summary: Velvet Ant backdoored the PAM and OpenSSH components that decide who signs in, planting access beyond the reach of routine cleanup. The group held persistence on an isolated network for roughly a decade with full view of administrator activity. Defenders should audit authentication binaries and login modules for tampering that endpoint tools miss.

sources.title: Chinese hackers hijack auth flow, spy on isolated network for a decade
China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade

topics.title: Anthropic Pulls Fable 5 and Mythos 5
topics.last_24h: Anthropic took its most advanced models offline worldwide after the Trump administration ordered it to block all foreign nationals from Fable 5 and Mythos 5.
topics.summary: The government issued the order at 5:21 p.m. ET on national-security grounds, citing export controls and a jailbreak concern. Anthropic is complying but disputes the basis, calling the cited jailbreak narrow and the capability widely available elsewhere. The suspension hits every user worldwide, since the company could not cleanly separate foreign access, and marks the first time export rules have grounded a frontier AI model.

sources.title: US Gov asks Anthropic to ban 'foreign national' access to Fable, Mythos
U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals
Anthropic Says It Has Taken Its Latest AI Models Offline to Comply With New Export Controls

topics.title: Section 702 Surveillance Lapse
topics.last_24h: Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act is poised to lapse for the first time since 2008 after Congress deadlocked.
topics.summary: Section 702 authorizes warrantless collection of foreign communications and has drawn years of privacy fights over incidental capture of Americans' data. A legislative stalemate now threatens to suspend the program, a first in its history. Intelligence agencies warn of operational gaps; privacy advocates see leverage for reform.

sources.title: Major US surveillance program poised to lapse after legislative deadlock

topics.title: Google Sues Gemini Smishing Network
topics.last_24h: Google sued a Chinese cybercrime network it accuses of weaponizing its Gemini AI agent to mass-produce phishing texts aimed at Americans.
topics.summary: Google says the group built and ran a phishing-as-a-service kit called Outsider and leaned on Gemini to generate and manage smishing campaigns. The lawsuit marks an aggressive move to police abuse of the company's own AI tools. It arrives as vendors and regulators confront criminals folding generative AI into established fraud operations.

sources.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Accused of Using Gemini AI in Phishing

topics.title: 23andMe Breach Settlement
topics.last_24h: A bankruptcy administrator approved a $47 million fund to compensate victims of the 23andMe data breach.
topics.summary: Hackers stole data on about 7 million customers of the genetics-testing company starting in April 2023, and much of it surfaced on dark-web forums. The approved settlement routes $47 million to affected customers as the bankrupt company winds down. The case underscores the lasting liability of holding sensitive genetic records.

sources.title: Bankruptcy admin approves settlement fund of $47 million for 23andMe data breach victims

sports: Marlins beat the Pirates 8-3 for their 6th straight
Next: Marlins @ Pirates  Sat Jun 13, 4:05 PM
Marlins try to extend road win streak in matchup against
2026 MLB ABS challenge system tracker: Team, player

business_politics.text: The United States and Iran moved to the verge of a deal to end their war, with Pakistan's prime minister saying both sides will sign within 24 hours and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Oil futures settled at multimonth lows and Treasury yields fell for the week on the prospect.

sources.title: Pakistan says US-Iran deal to be signed within 24 hours
Deal With Iran Is Close, but Accord Will Kickstart Toughest Phase of Talks

business_politics.text: SpaceX rose nearly a fifth on its trading debut after raising $75 billion in the largest initial public offering ever, a valuation of $2.1 trillion that made Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire. Bankers sold investors on a sci-fi strategy that overlooks steep losses and hands Musk full control.

sources.title: SpaceX’s surge on debut makes Musk world’s first trillionaire
SpaceX, Now Worth $2.1 Trillion, Pulls Off Goldilocks Debut

business_politics.text: US headline annual inflation reached 4.2%, the highest since April 2023, complicating any case for monetary easing and reviving debate over whether a wait-and-see Federal Reserve risks repeating 2008-era mistakes.

sources.title: Chart of the Week: US headline inflation matters to consumers
How to tame the inflation python

business.text: Allegheny County extended the public comment period on a proposal requiring every county business to provide 18 weeks of paid parental leave. Supporters call it overdue; some employers, including the county EMS council, warn it could push businesses out.

sources.title: Allegheny County extends public comment period for paid parental leave proposal

business.text: A Manor couple, Kelly and Jesse Dowdrick, launched WanderBrew, a mobile coffee shop built inside a 1978 trailer she spotted on Facebook Marketplace 32 minutes after pitching the idea.

sources.title: Manor couple launches WanderBrew coffee trailer

business.text: Polymath Park in Westmoreland County added a row of cedar dining pods tucked among the trees, selling seclusion as a draw for nature dining.

sources.title: Treehouse-inspired pods offer nature dining in Polymath Park

around_town.text: Harrisburg legislative leaders say they expect to pass the state budget on time, avoiding a repeat of last year's 135-day impasse that delayed billions of dollars to schools, counties, and nonprofits.

sources.title: Pa. lawmakers optimistic about passing budget on time as deadline looms

around_town.text: State troopers issued nearly 700 citations in the first week of enforcing Paul Miller's Law, Pennsylvania's ban on handheld devices behind the wheel.

sources.title: During first week of Paul Miller’s Law enforcement, troopers issue nearly 700 citations

around_town.text: Part of the Great Allegheny Passage in West Mifflin will close starting the evening of July 9 so contractors can scale rockfall hazards above the trail.

sources.title: Part of the Great Allegheny Passage is closing while contractors address rockfall hazards

around_town.text: A $6.8 million project on Stewart Avenue will reduce localized flooding and upgrade stormwater infrastructure across two Pittsburgh neighborhoods.

sources.title: $6.8M project aims to reduce flooding in 2 Pittsburgh neighborhoods

around_town.text: Educators across Western Pennsylvania split over how much screen time belongs in elementary classrooms, weighing scrolling as a skill against foundational reading, writing, and math.

sources.title: Debate surrounding elementary school screen time reverberates throughout Western Pa.

events.text: The Bantam Jeep Heritage Festival runs this weekend at Coopers Lake Campground in Butler County, drawing thousands of enthusiasts to honor the vehicle designed in Butler.

sources.title: Bantam Jeep Heritage Festival takes over Main Street in Butler

events.text: Westmoreland County hosts a slate of weekend happenings, including a Flag Day observance, an art show, and a fishing derby.

sources.title: Westmoreland happenings: Flag Day observance, art show, fishing derby, more

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: The Silicon Valley Bubble (Part 1)
reading.summary: Zitron argues the AI era is ending as OpenAI and Anthropic file to go public, racing for exit liquidity despite burning billions a year with no path to profitability.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: Fable 5, Anthropic Alignment, AI Tiers
reading.summary: Thompson examines Fable 5, the public version of Mythos, finding it very capable but setting troubling new precedents around alignment and tiered access to AI models.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Why Isn’t AI Taking Our Jobs?
reading.summary: Newport questions the AI industry's comparison of its technology to industrial automation, probing why predicted job losses have not materialized.
